heading: Appropriations for private organizations
label: 1310 Appropriations for private organizations
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised SectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)1310(a)31:721(words before 14th comma).(par. immediately before heading “Smithsonian Institution”), .June 23, 1874, ch. 455, § 118 Stat. 2161310(b)31:721(words between 14th comma and proviso).1310(c)31:721(proviso).In subsection (a), before clause (1), the words “by warrant” are omitted as unnecessary because of chapter 33 of the revised title. The word “appropriation” is substituted for “moneys appropriated” for consistency in the revised title. The words “for a private organization” are substituted for “for the aid, use, support, or benefit of any charitable, industrial, or other association, institution, or corporation” to eliminate unnecessary words. The word “official” is substituted for “officer” for consistency in the revised title. In clause (1), the word “Treasury” is substituted for “Treasurer of the United States” because of the source provisions restated in section 321 of the revised title and Department of the Treasury Order 229 of (39 F.R. 2280). The words “or of an assistant treasurer” in , are omitted as superseded by section 1(1st par. under heading “Independent Treasury”) of the Act of (ch. 214, ).January 14, 1974May 29, 1920section 1 of the Act of June 23, 187441 Stat. 254In subsection (b), before clause (1), the words “The fiscal official may pay an amount out of the appropriation” are substituted for “shall be paid out” for clarity. In clause (1), the words “for services, materials, or any other purpose” are omitted as unnecessary. In clause (2), the words “in writing” are omitted as surplus. The word “purpose” is substituted for “object or purpose” to eliminate unnecessary words.In subsections (b)(2) and (c), the word “amount” is substituted for “the avails thereof” for clarity.In subsection (c)(1), before clause (A), the words “an amount of less than $20 out of the appropriation” are substituted for “payments are to be made under $20” for clarity. In clause (B), the words “in writing on the check” are omitted as unnecessary.In subsection (c)(2), the word “Secretary” is substituted for “Treasurer” because of the source provisions restated in section 321(c) of the revised title.

children.text: a designated depositary of the United States Government (except a national bank).
payable to the order of the person to whom payment is to be made; and
that states the specific purpose for which the amount is to be applied.

children.label: A
children.text: payable to the order of the fiscal official; and

children.label: B
children.text: that states the amount is to be applied to small claims.

The fiscal official shall provide the Secretary or the designated depositary on which the check is drawn with a certified list of the claims. The list shall state the kind and amount of each claim and the name of each claimant.
